chunks.text: Felix F. Wu is Professor Emeritus at EECS, UC Berkeley. He received his BS from National Taiwan University (1965), MS from University of Pittsburgh (1969), and PhD from UC Berkeley (1972). He taught at Pittsburgh before joining UC Berkeley in 1974, progressing from Assistant to Professor Emeritus. From 1997-2000, he served as Pro Vice Chancellor (Research) at University of Hong Kong and Director of Graduate School.
chunks.question: What are Felix Wu's academic degrees and where did he earn them?
chunks.answer: BS from Taiwan, MS from Pittsburgh, PhD from Berkeley.

chunks.text: Currently, Felix Wu holds the Philip KH Wong Wilson KL Wong Professor in Electrical Engineering and Chair Professor at HKU. He directs HKU's Center for Electrical Energy Systems and the HKU-Tsinghua Shenzhen Power System Research Institute. He was awarded IEEE Fellow in 1989 for contributions to power system theory and computation. In 1991, he held the TEPCO Chair for future electric energy systems.
chunks.question: What positions does Felix Wu currently hold at HKU?
chunks.answer: Chair Professor and director of energy centers.

chunks.text: Felix Wu has been a Visiting Professor at ETH-Zurich, University of Tokyo, University of Cassino, Tsinghua University, and others. He served on editorial boards of professional journals and IEEE committees including Prize Paper and Power Systems Engineering. He consulted for industry and government agencies like Pacific Gas & Electric, EPRI, Chinese Electric Power Research Institute, ABB, Statkraft, Iberdrola, Manila Electric, and Taipower.
chunks.question: Which organizations has Felix Wu consulted for?
chunks.answer: PG&E, EPRI, Chinese EPRI, ABB, and others.

chunks.text: Prof. Wu is a Trustee of Croucher Foundation since 2004. He was a Board Member of Shantou University (2000-2003), Council Member of Hong Kong Productivity Council, and member of Hong Kong Government Electricity Appeal Board. He is a founding member of International Institute for Critical Infrastructures and served as its President from 2003-2005.
chunks.question: What leadership roles has Felix Wu held in foundations and institutes?
chunks.answer: Trustee of Croucher, President of Critical Infrastructures Institute.

chunks.text: Selected publication: A. S. Chuang, F. F. Wu, and P. Varaiya, 'A game-theoretic model for generation expansion planning: Problem formulation and numerical comparisons,' IEEE Trans. Power Systems, vol. 16, no. 4, pp. 885-891, Nov. 2001.
chunks.question: What is the title of Felix Wu's selected publication?
chunks.answer: Game-theoretic model for generation expansion planning.

chunks.text: Selected honors and awards: National Academy of Engineering Member (2026) and IEEE Fellow (1991).
chunks.question: What major honors has Felix Wu received?
